title: The Empty Cradle: How Falling Birthrates Threaten World Prosperity (And What To Do About It)
author: Phillip Longman
description: Phillip Longman's groundbreaking analysis of global fertility collapse and the economic, fiscal, and geopolitical crisis it will produce if current trends continue. He proposes a practical, cross-ideological policy agenda for reversing the decline.
rationale: Chosen as the foundational book for the demographics-and-population-studies leaf because it is the single most influential popular-press treatment of declining fertility published in the United States in the 2000s. It brought demographic decline from specialist journals into mainstream policy discourse, introduced the public to the concept of replacement fertility and its consequences, and advanced a policy agenda (pro-natalist family support) that continues to shape policy debate. Unlike purely academic demographic texts, Longman's trade-press writing and New America Foundation platform gave the argument broad reach. It is also an unconventional complement to the dominant environmentalist fixation on overpopulation that characterized the preceding decades of population discourse.
